name: Platform-Neutral Authentication Standards Adoption
consequences: Immediate: Mandating FIDO2/WebAuthn integration in public services → Systemic: 40% reduction in platform-specific authentication dependencies by 2029 (based on eIDAS2 adoption rates) → Strategic: Establishes technical precedent for supplier-neutral identity infrastructure, forcing platform vendors to adapt or lose public-sector access. Controls regulatory compliance vs. vendor lock-in. Weakness: The options fail to consider potential legal challenges from platform providers over API access restrictions.
options: Require all public-sector identity systems to adopt FIDO2/WebAuthn as a mandatory baseline standard by 2027
Create a certification pathway for platform-neutral authentication solutions within eIDAS2 compliance frameworks
Integrate blockchain-based attestation mechanisms into OpenID Connect protocols for decentralized trust verification
review: Controls regulatory compliance vs. vendor lock-in. Weakness: The options fail to consider potential legal challenges from platform providers over API access restrictions.

name: Regulatory Framework Strengthening
consequences: Immediate: Amending procurement laws to prohibit exclusive reliance on Apple/Google app-store distribution → Systemic: 25% increase in alternative identity solution bids by 2028 (based on EU digital market trends) → Strategic: Creates legal precedent for supplier diversification, compelling platform vendors to open up integrity signals or face market exclusion. Controls institutional inertia vs. innovation. Weakness: The options fail to consider resistance from public-sector procurement officers accustomed to legacy systems.
